events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: neutrals and grounds mixed in a subpanel. This is a code violation under NEC 200.4 (neutral conductors must not be shared or mixed improperly) and NEC Art. 408 (panelboard requirements). Neutrals and grounds should be separated in a subpanel (neutral bar isolated from ground bar). This is a safety hazard - can cause objectionable current on ground paths, shock/arc flash risk.

Decision: fail
Risk: high
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 60
Confidence: high, say 0.9

Findings: neutrals and grounds mixed on same bar in subpanel; neutral-ground bond not properly separated; objectionable current on equipment grounding conductors; potential shock/arc flash hazard.

Actions: separate neutral and grounding conductors onto their respective bars; verify neutral bar is isolated from ground in subpanel; re-terminate and torque to spec; re-inspect before energizing.

References: NEC Art. 408, NEC 200.4, NEC 110.26.

"Verify neutral-to-ground bond is removed in the subpanel; bond only at service or separately derived source",
"Re-terminate, torque to manufacturer specs, and verify continuity/isolation",
"Do not energize panel until corrected and re-inspected"
],
